Vendored JavaScript libraries for the chat markdown rendering pipeline.
These files are loaded by ``index.html`` over a same origin relative path so
the app keeps working behind reverse proxies with a stripped sub path
(e.g. Traefik stripprefix /atop) and without any runtime CDN call.

Any bump must:
  1. replace the file,
  2. update the ``version``, ``url`` and ``sha256`` fields below,
  3. re-run ``docker compose build`` to make sure the image ships the new
     asset, and
  4. re-run ``docker compose run --rm test pytest -q`` to keep the XSS
     regression test green.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------
name:    marked
version: 9.1.6
url:     https://registry.npmjs.org/marked/-/marked-9.1.6.tgz  (package/marked.min.js)
sha256:  6002af63485b043fa60ddaba1b34363b98d2a8b2c63b607004f3a2405a8a053a
notes:   Last line of 9.x ships a UMD bundle that exports ``window.marked``.
         10.x and later moved to ESM only, which would force us to rework
         the loader. Sticking with 9.1.6 keeps the ``<script>`` tag model.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------
name:    DOMPurify
version: 3.4.1
url:     https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/dompurify@3.4.1/dist/purify.min.js
sha256:  c3d1c163c700071ad546390cb92a36c31cd6aceaad1bf77c4ef6eb9d845f1976
notes:   UMD build; exposes ``window.DOMPurify``. We only use
         ``DOMPurify.sanitize(html)`` with the default (very conservative)
         allow list, so no ``config`` object is passed at call sites.
